    AI Overview
    In Q3 2025, the Egyptian real estate market witnessed a robust rebound driven by economic reforms, with
    Cairo's residential stock reaching ~317,000 units after 7,500 units were completed, largely in the New Administrative Capital (NAC). Property prices continue to surge, with developers reporting 10–30% increases, while some areas see massive appreciation. Demand remains high, particularly for luxury properties, with developers offering significant cash discounts to encourage sales, notes a WAYA media report. 
    Key Market Dynamics (Q3 2025): 
    • Residential Growth: 7,500 units were completed, mainly in the R7 district of the NAC. The market is shifting towards a more demand-driven, rational phase.
    • Significant Price Increases: In Nasr City, average apartment prices rose by 32.7% annually, reaching
      ∼tilde
      EGP
      (
      US
      ) per sqm, while villa prices grew by over 189%, according to Global Property Guide analysis.
    • Office Sector Performance: Around 71,600 sqm of office space was delivered, bringing the total stock to 2.5 million sqm. Limited prime space led to a 7.6% annual increase in asking rents.
    • Investor Sentiment: Real estate remains a key hedge against currency volatility. The market is witnessing high interest from local and international buyers, with a notable, sustained focus on the North Coast and Cairo.
    • Sales Performance: Some developers saw massive growth in demand, with Makadi Heights reporting Q3 sales reaching EGP 1.8 billion, a 42.4% increase over Q2 2025. 
    Trends and Future Outlook: 
    • Continued Price Growth: Property prices in 2025 are projected to rise between 10% and 30%, driven by sustained demand, high inflation, and construction costs.
    • Private Sector Development: Over 68.6% of residential projects are privately developed, underscoring high market confidence, notes a Property Finder Egypt analysis.
    • Strategic Focus: The market is favoring developments with strong infrastructure and amenities, particularly within new city developments like the NAC and, as shown on Elbayt.com, the North Coast.
    • Market Correction: Despite rising prices, some areas are experiencing a price correction, with developers offering unprecedented cash discounts exceeding 50% to boost sales and strengthen cash flow, notes WAYA.
